hook.text: If you've been told you need bowel surgery for endo watch this first
hook.target_audience_raw: patients who have been told they need bowel surgery for endometriosis

main_claim.text: Most bowel endometriosis cases are treated with shaving, as deep involvement through the bowel wall is rare.

supporting_explanation.summary: The video explains that a recto-end or anal ultrasound scan (available in Cardiff) can assess how deep endometriosis goes into the bowel wall. If it is only on the surface or just below the surface, a skilled laparoscopic surgeon can perform shaving. Full-thickness involvement (through the muscularis propria, called 'the barrier to poo') is rare and requires a colorectal surgeon. The scan provides valuable information for surgical planning.

funnel_rationale: The video educates patients already diagnosed with bowel endometriosis about treatment options (shaving vs. full-thickness resection) and specialist involvement (colorectal surgeon). This is mid-funnel content for those considering treatment choices.

secondary_raw: recto-end ultrasound scanning technique, shaving vs full-thickness resection, colorectal surgeon involvement

format_raw: video with onscreen text and spoken narration
